rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: PART 1 OF 2: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is John D. Minton and Asher Anderson's Motion To Quash Service ("Motion"). Per the Notice of Motion, Movants seek an order of this court under Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10(a)(1), quashing service of the notice of hearing and Second Amended Petition served upon them by mail on September 26, 2025. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10(a)(1), "A defendant, on or before the last day of his or her time to plead or within any further time that the court may for good cause allow, may serve and file a notice of motion for one or more of the following purposes: (1) To quash service of summons on the ground of lack of jurisdiction of the court over him or her." On September 29, 2025, Ms. Colarusso filed Second Amended Verified Petition For: Breach Of Fiduciary Duty-Constructive Fraud/Fraudulent Concealment Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Fiduciary Duty-Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Against Jon Vigna For Failure To Remedy Breaches Of Michael Rooney-Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Against Michael Rooney Sole Special Administrator/Co Executor On Surety Bond-Extrinsic Fraud-Restitution Surcharge Removal Of Executor And Appointment Of Successor To Set Aside Final Judgment ("Second Amended Petition"), naming Movants, among other parties, as Respondents and seeking money damages and restitution from them. The petition "requests that the Court exercise its jurisdiction under probate code section 1300, 1303 and 187 to Remove the Executor, surcharge fiduciaries, order restitution, set aside the void judgment and enforce liability against the Surety bond from the protection of the Estate and its heir's [sic]." (Pet. at 4:3-5.) The Motion is brought on the grounds that (1) the Second Amended Petition is an action in personum as to Movants, as it seeks monetary damages and equitable relief against them, (2) the notice of hearing was not accompanied by a summons, (3) Movants have not appeared in this matter in their personal capacities, but only as attorneys for other parties, and thus, this court does not yet have personal jurisdiction over Movants in this matter. A Joinder in the Motion was filed by Vincent Yu and Sheila Robello on November 4, 2025, stating neither Mr. Yu nor Ms. Robello were served with a summons, although they too received several documents from Ms. Colarusso by mail. No opposition to the Motion has been timely filed. The court notes, Petitioner filed Probate Ex Parte Application For Order Authorizing Issuance Of Summons And Authorizing Service And Acknowledgment Under CCP 415.30, with supporting declaration, on November 19, 2025. The Motion argues the Second Amended Petition is pleaded in such a way, and seeks such relief, as if it were a civil complaint and thus should procedurally operate by summons and complaint rather than through the Probate Code's procedures of petition and notice. The general rules of civil practice on procedural matters are applicable in probate proceedings unless the Probate Code provides a special rule. Where the Probate Code provides no special provisions for the circumstances presented, the civil statutes apply. (Prob. C. sec. 1000; see, e.g., Swaithes v. Superior Ct. (1989) 212 Cal. App. 3d 1082, 1084.) Here, the Second Amended Petition does not facially present - and Petitioner does not otherwise argue - that it is pleaded under a provision of the Probate Code or under circumstances that provides a special procedural rule. The general rules of civil practice apply. The Motion is GRANTED.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: PART 2 of 2 Movants shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA GANLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/21/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The third supplement filed 11/26/2025 requests a new personal representative and amends the previously filed a petition. For the court to make a ruling, an amended petition is required. 2) An amended pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading at amends (CRC 7.53(a)). 3) 1. No Duties and Liabilities has been filed (Probate Code Section 8404). 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all future filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 01/07/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT COURTNEY HAYDEN IV
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/27/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The addresses of the residuary trust beneficiaries have not been set forth, as they should have been had the residuary beneficiaries been included in item 8 of the petition, as required by the Judicial Council form and reiterated in LRSF 14.18 C.2. (2) No proof of service of notice to the (remainder) trust beneficiaries appears to have been filed. [Probate Code Section 1208(b).] (3) An incomplete proof of service was filed on 11/4/25 for a hearing on 1/10/26, not this hearing. (The correct proof of service is the one on the reverse side of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Petition to Administer Estate.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 1/13/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Lack of courtesy copies can delay review. Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in response to issues posted in a tentative ruling or to "Examiner's Notes." Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA J. CHU

rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 06/14/2027.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTURO JIMENEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The original will does not appear to have been filed. (2) The copy of the will attached to the petition appears to be incomplete. The last page is missing. (3) No proof of service of the notice has been filed. (4) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. (7) The date of death of any deceased heir or beneficiary must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 D.] (8) Item 1 of the petition appears to be untrue; notice was not published in the San Francisco Bay Guardian. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/20/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Items 3g(2) and 6 of the petition should not be completed when there is a will and when a decedent is survived by issue.)
